title: acer monspessulanum subsp. monspessulanum
intro: Acer monspessulanum subsp. monspessulanum, also known as the Montpellier maple, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to southern Europe and western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Acer monspessulanum subsp. monspessulanum include reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
